channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 Director Open to Explore DLC Possibilities
item.content:encoded:
The director of the Final Fantasy 7 Remake series has stated that he isn’t against the idea of DLC for Part 3.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 is a game that many players have been waiting for, especially since the first two installments appeared on Nintendo and Xbox platforms. It seems that fans might be more excited about this entry as a DLC hasn’t been ruled out.

Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 DLC is a Possibility If There’s Enough Interest

During a new interview with ntower (via GamesRadar+), Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 director Naoki Hamaguchi discussed DLC possibilities. Hamaguchi first explained why they didn’t release a DLC for the previous game, stating that they had “considered the possibility of developing a story DLC right up until immediately before the project officially moved into the planning phase.”

They ended up not going ahead with the idea, as they wanted to work on the third installment in parallel, to help with the release plans and quality of the third game. The main goal of the devs was “to deliver the third entry to fans as quickly as possible at a level of polish they would find satisfying.”

At the same time, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 could get DLC, as Naoki Hamaguchi stated that it depends on “how strongly fans continue to support Final Fantasy 7 as both a title and a franchise.” If the demand is enough, the team is “open to exploring those possibilities proactively.”

In other news, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 will feature new gameplay mechanics, and the director has already completed over 40 full playthroughs of the upcoming title. What are your thoughts on Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 DLC not being ruled out? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Nintendo being pressured to raise Switch 2 prices. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Is 007 First Light Canon In James Bond? 007 First Light Vs Bond Movies, Explained
item.content:encoded:
James Bond has one of the richest histories out there, with the character dating all the way back to the 1950s. Almost a decade later, Sean Connery undertook the role of the super spy in Dr. No, and 007 has been a cinema staple ever since.

His dominance didn’t stop at the big screen, as he’s had a ton of video game spin-offs, with 007 First Light being the latest. As you can imagine, it can be tough to keep up with the Bond canon, and IO Interactive’s upcoming game has thrown another spanner in the works with their brand new origin story.

It begs the question: is 007 First Light part of the James Bond canon, and is the game related to the films in any way? For all you Bond purists out there, hopefully this clears the air a little bit.

What Is The James Bond Canon?

Generally speaking, there have been two canon runs for James Bond, with the first 20 films all part of the same canon, whilst Daniel Craig’s movies served as a reboot for the franchise.

Whilst it might seem hard to wrap your head around, since there were five different Bond actors throughout the original film franchise, it’s best to think of them like a comic book. They all loosely tied into one another, but with different writers and artists throughout the years, telling one winding adventure.

  • More Insider Gaming: 007 First Light Cast Revealed—All Voice Actors, Explained

However, when Daniel Craig took the hot seat with Casino Royale in 2006, it served as a reboot for the Bond franchise, resetting the canon. All of Craig’s films are much stricter with continuity, as they flow seamlessly from one to the next.

Of course, now that it’s wrapped up, it’s unclear whether a new actor will step in and continue his story, or if we’ll see another Bond reboot. Pair that with 007 First Light coming out, and you can see where the confusion stems from: will the game fit into a prior canon, or could it maybe be the first step to succeeding Daniel Craig?

Is 007 First Light Canon In James Bond?

The simple answer is no, 007 First Light isn’t canon in the general James Bond lore, as the game doesn’t tie into the movies in any way.

Instead, IO Interactive is looking to tell its own unique James Bond story, with the game serving as an origin story. You’ll follow Bond as he begins as an MI6 recruit and eventually evolves into the 007 agent we all know him for.

  • More Insider Gaming: All 007 First Light Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained

Naturally, the developers have taken tons of inspiration from the plethora of source material, so in spirit it should feel like a story ripped straight from the classic Bond stories that you’ve grown to love—just adapted to a video game format.

Interestingly, it was originally discussed whether the game would be canonical when IOI had early talks with Amazon MGM, the owners of the Bond IP. Despite that, it was ultimately decided that the game would live alongside the future of 007, but not be overtly connected.

Were you hoping 007 First Light would be canonical to other James Bond entries? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more 007 First Light content, check out the following:

  • 007 First Light Game Pass & Early Access Status, Explained
  • 007 First Light Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
  • How Long Is 007 First Light? Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Subnautica 2 Early Access PC System Requirements—How Much Space Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Need?
item.content:encoded:
The ocean is about to be the biggest and best it’s ever been in a Subnautica game. New features are likely to require more power, though, so find out the minimum, recommended, ultra, and ultra++ PC specs and requirements in Subnautica 2 early access.

Yes, Subnautica 2 early access is also on Xbox, but I imagine the majority of players will be on PC. Previous games haven’t been too bad in terms of PC specs and requirements, but it’s a whole other kettle of ocean-living fish for Subnautica 2 EA.

Subnautica 2 Early Access Minimum Requirements on PC

Here’s the complete list of Subnautica 2 early access minimum PC specs:

  • GPU: Nvidia GTX 1660 / AMD RX 5500XT
  • CPU: Intel i5-8400 / AMD Ryzen 5 2600
  • OS: Windows 10/11
  • Memory: 12 GB
  • GPU Memory: 6 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1080p / Low
  • FPS: 30 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Recommended Requirements on PC

Now it’s time for the recommended requirements on PC, which should serve as a baseline for most players:

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 3070 / AMD RX 6700XT
  • CPU: Intel i7-13700 / AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 16 GB
  • GPU Memory: 8 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1440p / Medium
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Ultra Requirements on PC

Let’s step it up a notch now with Subnautica 2 early access Ultra requirements; asking more of your PC.

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 4070 / AMD RX 6900XT
  • CPU: Intel i7-13700 / AMD Ryzen 7 7700X
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 32 GB
  • GPU Memory: 12 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 1440p / High
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Ultra++ Requirements on PC

Now for the final boss: The Subnautica 2 early access Ultra++ PC specs for the cleanest deep-sea exploration:

  • GPU: Nvidia RTX 5070TI / AMD RX 7900XTX
  • CPU: Intel i9-14900 / AMD Ryzen 9 7900X3D
  • OS: Windows 11
  • Memory: 32 GB
  • GPU Memory: 16 GB
  • Resolution & Preset: 4K / High
  • FPS: 60 FPS

Subnautica 2 Early Access Storage Space Required

According to Steam, you need at least 50 GB of available space for Subnautica 2 early access.

To make room for Subnautica 2 early access and subsequent updates, you’ll need to free up 50 GB of storage space, if you haven’t already.

What are your first impressions of the Subnautica 2 system requirements, then? Too punishing for early access? Or, if your computer is a beast, feel free to let the Insider Gaming Discord community know about it!

We have more Subnautica 2 early access guides for you to check out:

  • Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Have Co-op or Multiplayer?
  • Subnautica 2 Early Access Xbox Game Pass Status, Explained
  • How Long Will Subnautica 2 Be In Early Access?
  • How Much Does Subnautica 2 Early Access Cost?
  • Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date, Times & All Platforms

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…
